text.title: Vision Basics — Provider + Input Mode Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: Is the image sensitive (PII / internal doc / customer screenshot)?

branch.question: After resize to 1024px, is post-base64 size <= 5 MB?

branch.conclusion: Provider = Anthropic Claude Sonnet (best at complex layouts + 200K context). Send as base64.
Reduce further: resize to 768px long edge, quality=75. If still > 5 MB, fall back to OpenAI GPT-4o (20 MB cap) with base64.

branch.question: Is this part of a high-volume batch (> 100 images / minute)?

branch.conclusion: Provider = Gemini Flash (cheapest per image, 1 M context, native batch). Apply cache aggressively.

branch.question: Does the downstream consumer require strict json_object enforcement?

branch.conclusion: Provider = OpenAI GPT-4o with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}`. URL input acceptable for public images.
Provider = Anthropic Claude Sonnet with schema-in-prompt + prefill `{`. URL input acceptable.

example.p: Input: invoice scan with PII, ~2 MB after resize. sensitive=true → post_b64_size ≤ 5 MB → conclusion r2-base64-for-sensitive: Claude Sonnet, base64 input. Cache by image hash + task + provider + model.
